evals.prompt: Build a Bubble Tea v2 view: a fixed 30-cell sidebar plus a flexible main pane filling the rest of the terminal width, with a header and footer. Use Lip Gloss.
rubric: JoinHorizontal/JoinVertical (or Place) string composition
sizes come from tea.WindowSizeMsg, not hardcoded
measures with lipgloss.Width/Size, not len()
main width = total width - sidebar width
no invented constraint/flex solver

evals.prompt: Build a selectable, scrollable list using the bubbles list component, with key navigation and a selection action.
rubric: imports charm.land/bubbles/v2/list
nests list.Model in the parent model
m.list, cmd = m.list.Update(msg) then returns the reassigned model
forwards tea.WindowSizeMsg down to the list
no hand-rolled scrolling when list suffices; no list mutation outside Update

evals.prompt: Build a scrollback log view that auto-follows the bottom but pins when the user scrolls up. Use the bubbles viewport.
rubric: viewport.Model created with viewport.New(WithWidth/WithHeight)
checks AtBottom() before deciding to follow
GotoBottom() only when following
SetContent on update
no unconditional GotoBottom; no manual ring buffer; no v1 high-performance-render / YPosition path

evals.prompt: Stream tokens from a goroutine into a v2 viewport: append and re-render, auto-following the bottom.
rubric: goroutine calls p.Send(deltaMsg) per delta
stream kicked off via a captured *tea.Program (or a Cmd)
Update appends + viewport.SetContent
GotoBottom gated on AtBottom()/a follow flag
never mutates the model or calls Update/viewport.Update from the goroutine; no blocking in Update

evals.prompt: Author a custom v2 component that follows Model/Update/View and nests inside a parent model.
rubric: New() constructor + a Model whose Update returns (Model, tea.Cmd)
View() returns a string for use as a child
parent routes msgs/sizes down and batches cmds up
side effects go through a Cmd, not direct IO in Update
no package-global mutable state

evals.prompt: Truncate and wrap a string containing CJK characters and emoji to a fixed column width without breaking the layout or ANSI escapes.
rubric: uses x/ansi (Truncate/Wrap/Hardwrap) or lipgloss width helpers
measures with ansi.StringWidth/lipgloss.Width over whole strings
accounts for wide characters / graphemes
no len() or rune count for width; no byte-index slicing; does not break ANSI escapes

evals.prompt: Set up a v2 program with alt-screen and mouse enabled, with Run() error handling and clean terminal restore on panic.
rubric: tea.NewProgram + p.Run() with the returned error checked
alt-screen and mouse set as tea.View fields (AltScreen, MouseMode)
relies on the runtime's panic recovery / handles ErrProgramPanic
os.Exit(1) on error
no tea.WithAltScreen()/WithMouseCellMotion() options; no manual enable-raw-mode juggling

evals.prompt: Regression check: generated v2 code must not contain v1 / github.com-beta patterns.
rubric: grep finds none of: a github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea OR github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss OR github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles import (the whole framework stack is charm.land/*/v2 — no mixing), positional viewport.New(w, h) instead of viewport.New(viewport.WithWidth(w), viewport.WithHeight(h)), View() string, tea.WithAltScreen()/WithMouseCellMotion() options, a KeyMsg struct using .Type/.Runes, space matched as " ", a MouseMsg struct using .Action/.Button, model mutation from a goroutine/View/Cmd body, len() for display width, or tea.Listen (Init() tea.Cmd is correct v2 and must NOT be flagged)
